pipeline_analysis.interpretation: II=1 permits consecutive read-request issue when addresses are sequential and the AXI master supports outstanding requests. It does not imply consecutive write transactions because write data production and burst formation can delay AW/W activity. Write spacing is estimated as max(AXI beats, useful elements times achieved II), with pipeline depth included before the first dependent write.

notes_about_assumptions: LLVM IR trace is the source of executed logical accesses and dynamic order.
HLS interface/csynth metadata supplies AXI width, burst limits, outstanding settings, and loop II when available.
Physical transactions are inferred from aligned addresses, continuity, width, and burst limits; no RTL waveform is used.
Issue/completion values are logical estimates, not RTL cycle-accurate timestamps.
Timeline lane order is not a data dependency. Only explicit graph edges are dependencies.
